title: crataegus oxyacantha var. incisa
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. incisa, also known as the cut-leaved hawthorn, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family native to Europe and western Asia. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with thorny branches and clusters of small, white or pale pink flowers that are rich in flavonoids and other medicinal compounds. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha var. incisa include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for heart health. The health benefits of this plant include reduced risk of heart disease, improved circulation, and lower cholesterol levels, and it can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, making it an accessible addition to a healthy lifestyle.
